name: Alto de Malagón y de la Cruz Verde - por San Lorenzo de El Escorial — circular por el Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama
region_intro: Road cycling around Las Navas Del Marqués is characterized by its location in the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama, with elevations reaching up to 1600 meters. The region features extensive pine and oak forests, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Numerous reservoirs, lakes, and rivers contribute to the scenic environment. This area offers a range of road cycling routes, from moderate to challenging, often utilizing quiet country roads. Best road cycling routes around Las Navas Del Marqués The most popular road cycling route is Roads of the Sierra de Guadarrama — circular from Navalperal de Pinares, a 63.1 miles (101.5 km) trail that takes 5 hours 26 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alto de Malagón y de la Cruz Verde - por San Lorenzo de El Escorial — circular por el Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama, a difficult 46.8 miles (75.2 km) path. This route offers scenic views within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto de la Lancha loop from Las Navas del Marqués, a 31.3 miles (50.4 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Road cycling around Las Navas Del Marqués is defined by pine forests, mountain passes, and reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Las Navas Del Marqués's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Las Navas Del Marqués?
A: Las Navas Del Marqués offers a diverse selection of over 10 road cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 4 moderate and 9 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable ride for most cyclists.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Las Navas Del Marqués?
A: The region is ideal for road cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. The sweeping roads and high passes of the Sierra de Gredos, which often feature in the Vuelta a España, are particularly enjoyable during these seasons. While summer can be warm, the higher elevations offer some respite, and winter can bring challenging conditions in the mountains.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Las Navas Del Marqués?
A: While many routes in Las Navas Del Marqués feature significant elevation gains, there are moderate options available. For those seeking a less strenuous ride, the area's quiet country roads offer a pleasant experience. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38265120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Lancha loop from Las Navas del Marqués&lt;/a&gt; are classified as moderate and can be a good starting point, offering varied landscapes without extreme difficulty.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Las Navas Del Marqués?
A: Road cycling around Las Navas Del Marqués is characterized by its location in the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama, with elevations reaching up to 1600 meters. You'll encounter extensive pine and oak forests, varied terrain with significant elevation gains, and quiet country roads. The region also features numerous reservoirs, lakes, and rivers, adding to the picturesque scenery.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Las Navas Del Marqués is renowned for its challenging routes, with 9 out of 13 routes classified as difficult. Experienced riders can tackle routes with significant elevation gains, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063939176/roads-of-the-sierra-de-guadarrama-circular-from-navalperal-de-pinares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roads of the Sierra de Guadarrama — circular from Navalperal de Pinares&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 100 km with more than 2000 meters of ascent. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063932655/alto-de-malagon-y-de-la-cruz-verde-por-san-lorenzo-de-el-escorial-circular-por-el-parque-nacional-de-la-sierra-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Malagón y de la Cruz Verde - por San Lorenzo de El Escorial&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass through areas rich in natural beauty. You might encounter features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3005039" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4219128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Fraile Rock Formation&lt;/a&gt;, and various reservoirs such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2729700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Aceña Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6162407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its extensive pine and oak forests, offering a refreshing natural environment.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or towns to explore while road cycling?
A: Yes, the area is rich in historical charm. Las Navas del Marqués itself boasts the 16th-century Castillo-Palacio de Magalia and the historic Iglesia de San Juan Bautista. Many routes offer views or pass near historical towns. The broader region includes the UNESCO World Heritage city of Ávila, known as the 'City of Stones and Saints,' and El Barco de Ávila with its 12th-century walls.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Las Navas Del Marqués are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063939176/roads-of-the-sierra-de-guadarrama-circular-from-navalperal-de-pinares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roads of the Sierra de Guadarrama — circular from Navalperal de Pinares&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38265120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Lancha loop from Las Navas del Marqués&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find scenic viewpoints on road cycling routes?
A: The region is known for its magnificent viewpoints. Specific passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1771270" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Malagón&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3148581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz Verde Pass&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning panoramas. You can also find viewpoints overlooking reservoirs, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5682647" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de las Bereas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which provides views of the Valtravieso Reservoir.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Las Navas Del Marqués?
A: The road cycling routes in Las Navas Del Marqués are well-regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 2.5 stars. Over 490 visitors have explored the area, often praising the varied terrain, the challenge of the mountain passes, and the serene beauty of the pine forests and reservoirs.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or cafes along the routes?
A: While specific cafes are not listed for every route, the region's towns and villages, including Las Navas del Marqués itself and nearby San Lorenzo de El Escorial, offer various establishments where cyclists can find refreshments. Many routes pass through or near these settlements, providing opportunities for a break.

Q: Is parking available near the starting points of road cycling routes?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in the towns and villages that serve as common starting points for these routes, such as Las Navas del Marqués, Navalperal de Pinares, or San Lorenzo de El Escorial. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

The Monastery of El Escorial was built by King Philip II in the 16th century to commemorate a military victory and serve as a royal pantheon for the Habsburg dynasty.
Designed by architects Juan Bautista de Toledo and Juan de Herrera, it is characterized by its sober, Renaissance style. Today it is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
